- Glycolic peel: Suitable for conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, fine lines and wrinkles, razor bumps, stretch marks, and general toning and texture improvement.
- Lactic peel: Recommended for actinic keratosis, dark circles under eyes, dry skin, eczema, dermatitis, melasma, psoriasis, razor bumps, scarring, sensitive skin, stretch marks, sun damage, and general toning and texture improvement.
- Salicylic peel: Effective for acne, acne scars, adolescent skin, large pores and blackheads, oily skin, psoriasis, razor bumps, and warts.
- BHA+ peel: Beneficial for acne, adolescent skin, large pores and blackheads, oily skin, psoriasis, razor bumps, and warts.

By considering your specific skin concerns, you can choose the peel that will best address your needs.

Argan Oil - 4 oz. Our Moroccan Argan Oil is 100% pure and free from additives and preservatives. It is packaged in a high-quality amber glass bottle, ensuring its freshness and longevity. The Argan Tree, scientifically known as Argania spinosa from the Sapotaceae family, is native to Morocco and can live for up to 150-200 years. For centuries, Berber women in Morocco have cherished the secret of using traditional cosmetic products made from Argania spinosa to protect their skin, hair, and nails from the harsh and dry weather conditions. Argan oil is considered one of the rarest oils in the world. To create our cosmetic-grade Argan oil, we carefully press and filter freshly harvested kernels from the Argan tree's fruit using an organic cotton sieve, resulting in a highly aromatic oil. This oil is easily absorbed by the skin and is commonly used for treating dermatologic conditions such as acne. In Europe, Argan oil has been widely used in the Aromatherapy industry for a long time, but it remains relatively unknown in the United States. Argan oil contains almost twice as much vitamin E or a-tocopherol as olive oil and is also rich in linoleic acids. Its well-documented anti-aging properties, thanks to its high levels of vitamin E and skin-softening saponins, make it a valuable addition to any skincare routine. Tocopherols play a crucial role in promoting human health due to their antioxidant properties and ability to combat free radicals.

To determine the expiration date, you can refer to the lot code. The lot code consists of six to seven digits and always begins with a 0, 1, 2, or 3. By reading the lot code, you can extract valuable information. The first three digits represent the day of the year when the product was manufactured, also known as the Julian Date. Following that, the next two digits indicate the year of production, while the last two digits serve as an internal batch number used for identification purposes. For instance, if you come across the lot code 0400901, it signifies that the product was made on February 9, 2009, and belongs to Batch Number 1.
